input._widthJustification: Set to L/2 = 650 km to match Nimbus's L/W=2 subduction-megathrust aspect-ratio assumption (seismicTsunami.ts ruptureAspectRatio()). The historical Sumatra W was ~200 km but Nimbus's app-level model uses the aspect-ratio-derived W; the GeoClaw fixture is comparing against Nimbus's source, not the historical seafloor.

publishedReference.label: Cocos Island deep-water
publishedReference.source: Bernard et al. 2006 Phil. Trans. R. Soc. A 364

_metadata.tolerationRationale: Per-source-class default for seismic-megathrust. See scripts/geoclaw/run_scenario.py DEFAULT_TOLERANCE_BY_TYPE for the rationale (Synolakis 2008 inter-model spread + closed-form vs 2D-AMR mismatch + source-model scatter).
_metadata.comparisonStrategy: geoclawComparison.test.ts feeds the fixture input to deriveSource() (Tier-1 closed form) → simulateSaintVenant1D in radial geometry → Heidarzadeh-Satake dispersion factor; asserts ±tolerance vs each geoclawProbes peak.
